output: Hey! ThreatClaw AI Ops at your service. How can I help with your security?

The Bloom filter is a probabilistic in-memory data structure (~580 KB) that checks in 200 nanoseconds if an IoC is known. It's loaded at boot from threat feeds and checks each incoming log against ~5000 IoCs. Zero false negatives guaranteed.

Each asset gets a risk score (0-100) based on: critical findings ×30, high ×15, medium ×5, active alerts ×20, kill chain +25, known exploit +20. Global score combines worst asset (60%) and average (40%), inverted on 100. ML can adjust based on observed behavior.

[tool_call: get_recent_alerts, params: {level: "high"}]
Searching for high alerts...

[tool_call: get_asset_info, params: {query: "DC"}]
Looking up domain controller...

Dashboard > Skills > Inventory > Active Directory. Enter LDAP URL (ldap://192.168.1.10:389 or ldaps://...:636), bind DN (e.g., CN=svc-threatclaw,CN=Users,DC=corp,DC=local), and password. ThreatClaw imports computers, users, groups and OUs automatically.

ThreatClaw supports 9 sector profiles: healthcare (×1.5), finance (×1.4), industry (×1.3), energy (×1.4), transport (×1.2), retail (×1.1), government (×1.2), education (×1.1), services (×1.0). Each profile adapts priority MITRE techniques and applicable compliance frameworks.

After detecting a critical incident, ThreatClaw proposes actions via HITL: block an IP on your firewall, disable an account in your directory, or open a ticket in your ticketing tool. The analyst approves via their configured channel or the dashboard. Action is verified (cryptographic nonce, identity, rate limit, protected assets) then executed. Everything is audited.
